summary: Stealth-persistent game-cheat loader disguised as a 'Mobile Control Panel'
details: On load of the package's main entry (launcher.js), the code installs an HKCU Run-key entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at a sibling file 'OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs' which silently re-launches `node launcher.js` window-hidden via wscript, and sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` to impersonate a legitimate Windows process. The same file then `eval`s an 86KB obfuscator.io-packed bundle (server.obf.js) read from disk, and polls Chrome window titles every 3 seconds via PowerShell (-EP Bypass) for a 'YouTube' tab as a covert activation gate. The cli.js `update` subcommand runs `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe` against every such process on the host before reinstalling itself globally — destructive on developer/CI machines and consistent with covering tracks. package.json advertises the package as 'Sensivity - Mobile Control Panel', but the shipped UI (public/index.html) and the 6.8MB native module (sens.node, exporting strings 'Teleport','LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head','Aimbot','Triggerbot') reveal a Roblox-style game-cheat panel; the description is a cover story. The combination of stealth persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name, eval of an obfuscated payload, browser-state-gated activation, indiscriminate process termination, and deceptive package metadata is a malware-distribution shape, not a quality issue.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
